notes: SHIP v1.14.304 - 'Black-at-rest' Command glow cleanup (web-Claude design drop, owner 'go with .304, ship it'). NO ship-note in the zip; change derived by DIFFING their build vs live .303 - their base == live .303 exactly (has N-10 + black-glass + Stage 2), so NOT a collision, a clean forward stack. Applied the 7 CSS deltas on top of live (new lines pulled from their file matched by selector, old lines matched exactly-once in live - zero transcription) + version bump. Change (redesign-scoped surfaces, ?legacy=1 byte-identical, NO JS touched): deepens the one-emitter/idle-dark doctrine ('glow only when it means something') - (1) STRIPS the inner accent glow (inset 0 0 26px -4px color-mix(--accent 14-20%)) from #pg-cmd .lens, .stat, .nba, .spill and the Crash Cart/Work .rf-card; (2) DIMS their borders (.lens/.stat cyan 36%->15%, .nba violet 42%->16%); (3) rewrites .spill box-shadow to rim+drop only (rim color rgba(255,255,255,.06)->rgba(205,235,245,.06)); (4) NEW rule #pg-cmd .tile:not(.hot):not(.hot-teal):not(.hot-gold){box-shadow: rim + drop, NO glow} at L9184 so RESTING telemetry tiles carry no glow - only .hot/.hot-teal/.hot-gold (accent --tc) emit (coexists with the .292 body.rd tile override L11522); (5) removes the inner-cyan-glow line from the .292 hero-lens machined-ring box-shadow (~L11838). Channel COLORS unchanged (cyan/violet stay); nothing locked touched (no 3D scene). Gates: braces 11795->11796 (+1 = the new resting-tile rule) balanced, CRLF preserved, three-stamp .303->.304; black-glass + N-06 Stage 2 + N-10 markers confirmed intact. DEVICE-VERIFY (John - aesthetic gate): Command reads FLATTER at rest - lens/stat/NBA lose their inner accent glow + have dimmer borders, resting shift-tiles are dark (no glow), and ONLY the hot/attention tiles (accent --tc) still emit; HANDOFF still amber, channel colors unchanged; Crash Cart/Work cards lose their inner --rfac glow (rim + drop only). Confirm it reads as intended 'black-at-rest', not washed-out/unreadable. NOTE: 3 CSS-only ships now stacked UNVERIFIED (.302 black-glass, .303 N-10, .304 black-at-rest) - one device pass covers all three. NEXT: N-11 Work Active-Workspace.
